channel.title: Miguel's Morning Bytes
channel.description: A daily executive briefing on the news shaping AI, enterprise software, and the future of work. Each morning, Miguel's Morning Bytes walks through the top stories from across the press — frontier model launches, competitor moves, Salesforce and Agentforce headlines, hiring and labor shifts — through the lens of what a Chief Revenue Officer needs to know.
channel.itunes:author: Miguel's Morning Bytes
channel.itunes:summary: A daily executive briefing on the news shaping AI, enterprise software, and the future of work. Each morning, Miguel's Morning Bytes walks through the top stories from across the press — frontier model launches, competitor moves, Salesforce and Agentforce headlines, hiring and labor shifts — through the lens of what a Chief Revenue Officer needs to know.

item.title: July 15, 2026 — IBM Suffers Biggest Share Drop in Its History as AI Crowds Out Traditional IT Spend
item.description: Today's brief leads with IBM Suffers Biggest Share Drop in Its History as AI Crowds Out Traditional IT Spend. Also covered: New York Becomes First US State to Suspend Data Centre Development; DeepMind Chief Demis Hassabis Calls for US-Led Body to Test Frontier AI Models; Chipmaker CXMT Seeks $10bn in Largest China IPO Since 2010; SoftBank's Son Ridicules AI Critics for 'Spitting Upwards'; Oracle Leads Race to Supply Japan With Top-Secret Cloud Services.

item.title: July 14, 2026 — Nvidia halves Asia buyer list in China chip crackdown
item.description: Today's brief leads with Nvidia halves Asia buyer list in China chip crackdown. Also covered: Trillion-Dollar Chip Rout Trains Spotlight on TSMC and ASML Results; Nearly 200 Economists and Tech Leaders Warn of A.I. Threats; Stolen laptops, data breaches, secret moles: the wildest claims in Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI; SoftBank's Masayoshi Son Sees Fusion as Key to AI's Future; DeepSeek's Liang Tops Amodei and Brockman as Richest AI Founder.

item.title: July 13, 2026 — Apple's 'Thermonuclear' Response to the OpenAI Threat
item.description: Today's brief leads with Apple's 'Thermonuclear' Response to the OpenAI Threat. Also covered: Meta's Louisiana Data Center to Surpass $250 Billion Price Tag; Xi to Debut at China's Flagship AI Summit as US Rivalry Heats Up; Companies Turn to Chinese AI Models to Cut Costs; OpenAI, Meta, xAI Compete for More Cost-Efficient AI Models; Alphabet Stock: Looking Beyond the $4.7 Billion EU Fine.

item.title: July 10, 2026 — OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Fidji Simo to Step Down
item.description: Today's brief leads with OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Fidji Simo to Step Down. Also covered: SK Hynix Raises $26.5 Billion in Biggest Foreign US Debut; EU Warns Meta Over Facebook and Instagram's Addictive Design; Meta Launches Muse Spark AI Model, Introduces First Paid Tier; OpenAI and Google Sell AI Models to Blacklisted Chinese Groups; Amazon CTO Says Companies Shifting to Cheaper Open-Source AI Models.

item.title: July 9, 2026 — Zhipu Surges 22% After $4 Billion Share Sale Priced at Low End
item.description: Today's brief leads with Zhipu Surges 22% After $4 Billion Share Sale Priced at Low End. Also covered: Meta to Build First Data Center in Canada, Expanding Global Fleet; China to Let AI Firms Buy Nvidia H200s, Information Says; Microsoft's Brad Smith on Washington's AI Policy: 'Regulation Without Transparent or Complete Rules'; Tech CEOs Ditch the AI Jobs Apocalypse Narrative; Research: AI Is Changing What Employers Want From New Hires.

item.title: July 8, 2026 — OpenAI to Roll Out Top AI Model Globally After Limited Preview
item.description: Today's brief leads with OpenAI to Roll Out Top AI Model Globally After Limited Preview. Also covered: China Says It Has Found Security Vulnerabilities in Anthropic's Claude Code; Meta Releases First Image Model Since Zuckerberg's AI Overhaul; Mistral AI Releases Robotics Model to Support Physical AI Push; Tech Used to Be a Mecca for Young Talent — Now, AI Start-Ups Are Hiring Fewer Entry-Level Talent in Favor of Older Workers With Top Degrees; Accenture and Google Cloud Launch Agentic AI Suite for Midmarket Companies Under $3B in Revenue.

item.title: July 7, 2026 — Samsung Posts Record Profit but Shares Tumble 10%, Triggering Global Chip Selloff
item.description: Today's brief leads with Samsung Posts Record Profit but Shares Tumble 10%, Triggering Global Chip Selloff. Also covered: Microsoft to Cut More Than 3,000 Jobs in Xbox as It Reprioritizes AI Spend; TeraWulf Signs $19 Billion Lease With Anthropic for AI-Infrastructure Campus; ECB and ESRB Warn Banks on AI-Driven Cyber Attacks From Frontier Models; DeepSeek Developing Its Own AI Chip; AI Legal Startup Norm Valued at $1.2 Billion in Funding Round.

item.title: July 5, 2026 — Nvidia Supplier Hon Hai's Sales Jump 40% on Continued AI Demand
item.description: Today's brief leads with Nvidia Supplier Hon Hai's Sales Jump 40% on Continued AI Demand. Also covered: Semiconductor Stocks Slide Amid AI Spending Concerns; Are the 'MANGOS' Stocks Already Turning Soft?; India Tells Meta to Remove Child Abuse Content from Instagram; The CEO Who Vowed to 'Fire Anyone Who Doesn't Use AI' Admits the Technology Can't Replace Her Executive Assistant; Philosophers Are the Latest Hiring Target for AI Companies.

item.title: July 3, 2026 — Microsoft Commits $2.5B to New 'Frontier' Enterprise AI Unit With 6,000 Forward-Deployed Engineers
item.description: Today's brief leads with Microsoft Commits $2.5B to New 'Frontier' Enterprise AI Unit With 6,000 Forward-Deployed Engineers. Also covered: Software Loans Hit Rough Patch Even Before AI Fears Triggered SaaS-Pocalypse; Alibaba, Tencent Join $2.8 Billion Funding for Kling AI; Read the Emails Revealing How Anthropic's Pentagon Relationship Fell Apart; Trump Says He Wants AI Guardrails, But 'As Little as Possible'; The AI Trade Is Losing One of Its Key Signals.

item.title: July 2, 2026 — OpenAI Proposes Giving the US Government a 5% Stake
item.description: Today's brief leads with OpenAI Proposes Giving the US Government a 5% Stake. Also covered: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine; CoreWeave, Nebius Tumble as Meta Stands to Become Fresh Cloud Threat; SoftBank Plans to Rent AI Compute in US at 10-Gigawatt Scale; Nvidia Offers Revenue Sharing Model for Aspiring AI Startups; Apple Lobbies US to Buy Chinese-Made Memory Chips.

item.title: July 1, 2026 — US Lifts Export Restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI Model
item.description: Today's brief leads with US Lifts Export Restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI Model. Also covered: Abu Dhabi's MGX Raises $49 Billion for One of the Biggest Ever AI Funds; Anthropic launches Claude Science in push for pharma revenue; 'Kill switches' could be needed for AI-powered trading, BoE official says; Microsoft is reportedly planning thousands of layoffs as it spends on AI; Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Show AI Impact on Labor.

item.title: June 30, 2026 — BIS Warns $1 Trillion AI Investment Boom Risks 'Protracted Bust'
item.description: Today's brief leads with BIS Warns $1 Trillion AI Investment Boom Risks 'Protracted Bust'. Also covered: Magnificent Seven Stocks Shed $2.3tn in Wall Street Tech Rotation; Heavy Corporate AI Spenders Add Staff Faster Than Peers; Anthropic Holds Back Mythos AI Over Cyber Risk; Weak Hiring Is Hurting Young Workers More Than AI, St. Louis Fed Finds; Ford Rehired 350 Veteran 'Gray Beard' Engineers After AI Quality Tools Fell Short.

item.title: June 29, 2026 — Samsung, SK to Spend $880 Billion to Drive Korea's AI Lead
item.description: Today's brief leads with Samsung, SK to Spend $880 Billion to Drive Korea's AI Lead. Also covered: AI fuels record $200bn M&A boom in US power sector; Austria Lobbies EU to Host Anthropic After US Access Curbs; Prompts Are The New Malware As Enterprise AI Defenses Fall Behind; A.I. 'Employees' Might Disrupt Work in Unexpected Ways; Is an AI Jobs Apocalypse Coming? Three Economists Square Off.

item.title: June 28, 2026 — AI 'exuberance' risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
item.description: Today's brief leads with AI 'exuberance' risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns. Also covered: Google caps Meta's Gemini use as AI demand strains capacity; China Has Matched Anthropic in Cybersecurity, Resetting AI Race; Google Confirms Massive Gemini Upgrade: AI Can Now Use Your PC; Apple Seeks US Approval to Buy Chips From Blacklisted CXMT; Digging Out From The AI Money Pit.

item.title: June 27, 2026 — Trump Administration Rolls Back Part of Anthropic Model Ban
item.description: Today's brief leads with Trump Administration Rolls Back Part of Anthropic Model Ban. Also covered: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.6 to Select Users Vetted by US Government; Apple's Vision Pro and Smart Glasses Chief to Join OpenAI; DeepSeek Plans Hiring Spree in Escalation of China's AI Talent War; The 33-year-old executive Satya Nadella is trusting to fix Microsoft's Copilot AI assistant; Cost of the AI Boom: Anxiety, Longer Hours.

item.title: June 26, 2026 — Apple Raises Mac and iPad Prices 20% on AI-Driven Memory Shortage
item.description: Today's brief leads with Apple Raises Mac and iPad Prices 20% on AI-Driven Memory Shortage. Also covered: OpenAI Leans Toward Delaying IPO Until 2027; Trump Administration Asks OpenAI to Stagger Release of GPT 5.6; Chinese AI Models Gain Ground on Anthropic and OpenAI; Pentagon Expands AI Role in Military Targeting; Microsoft Stock Suffers Historic June Rout as Investors Balk at AI Capex.

item.title: June 25, 2026 — Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of 'Illicitly' Accessing Claude AI Models
item.description: Today's brief leads with Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of 'Illicitly' Accessing Claude AI Models. Also covered: Micron Soars After AI-Fueled Forecast Shatters Estimates; Big Companies Aim to Ease AI Transition for American Workers; OpenAI, Broadcom Develop Custom Chip for AI Inference; Google Poised to Lose Two More Senior AI Staffers to Anthropic; Qualcomm to Acquire AI Software Firm Modular in $3.9 Billion Stock Deal.

item.title: June 23, 2026 — Global Tech Selloff Deepens as AI Rally Cools, SpaceX Sheds $400bn
item.description: Today's brief leads with Global Tech Selloff Deepens as AI Rally Cools, SpaceX Sheds $400bn. Also covered: Oracle Sheds 21,000 Jobs as It Continues AI-Focused Streamlining; China Takes Supercomputer Crown From U.S. For First Time Since 2017; Qualcomm Near Deal for AI Software Firm Modular at ~$4B; Trump Seeks to Boost Quantum Computing With New Executive Orders; ADP CEO: Stop Worrying About Jobs, Start Thinking About Tasks.

item.title: June 22, 2026 — Microsoft's Satya Nadella: We Can't Let AI Giants Eat the Economy
item.description: Today's brief leads with Microsoft's Satya Nadella: We Can't Let AI Giants Eat the Economy. Also covered: Is China Closing the A.I. Gap Faster Than Expected?; Getty Images Soars 200% in Early Trading After OpenAI Deal; Bain tests software takeover targets by vibecoding AI replicas; Tencent Tests AI Assistant for Its Super App WeChat in China; Microsoft and Chevron Sign 20-Year Power Deal For Texas Data Center.

item.title: June 21, 2026 — Samsung Electronics Deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to Global Workforce
item.description: Today's brief leads with Samsung Electronics Deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to Global Workforce. Also covered: Visa Connects with ChatGPT to Let AI Agents Automatically Make Purchases; AWS Says AI Agents Lack Business Context and Security, Launches Two Services to Patch the Gaps; Workers Are Emerging as the Next Big AI Logjam; Merz: Germany Loses 10,000–14,000 Industrial Jobs a Month; Galán Overhauls Iberdrola with a New Organizational Structure.

item.title: June 20, 2026 — Nobel Winner John Jumper to Leave Google DeepMind for Anthropic
item.description: Today's brief leads with Nobel Winner John Jumper to Leave Google DeepMind for Anthropic. Also covered: Trump Tells Axios He Doesn't See Anthropic as US Security Threat; UnitedHealth's $3 Billion AI Push Has Bots Calling Doctors; Lutnick's Anthropic Crackdown Claims New Power Over AI Models; Did Anthropic Talk Its Way Into an AI Export Ban?; EY: We Found Your Biggest AI Blind Spot. It's Called the 'Tempo Gap'.

item.title: June 18, 2026 — Amazon in Talks to Sell Custom AI Chips in Bid to Cut Nvidia's Dominance
item.description: Today's brief leads with Amazon in Talks to Sell Custom AI Chips in Bid to Cut Nvidia's Dominance. Also covered: Google DeepMind unveils plan to protect itself from its own rogue AI agents; Meta's AI gamble: Dina Powell McCormick opens door to Wall Street; Anthropic Lays Out Vision for How to Bolster AI Models' Safety; JPMorgan Chase cuts off Anthropic access for its Hong Kong staff; Microsoft, Amazon Cloud Services Face Tough EU Antitrust Law.

item.title: June 17, 2026 — OpenAI's Backers Spent $7.6M to Destroy a State Legislator. Anthropic Spent $10M to Rescue Him
item.description: Today's brief leads with OpenAI's Backers Spent $7.6M to Destroy a State Legislator. Anthropic Spent $10M to Rescue Him. Also covered: State Farm's AI Plan for Sales Agents Sparks Uproar. 'A Real Slap in the Face.'; ECB's Lagarde Warns AI Is Huge Risk for Financial Stability; Macron Seeks Way Around Trump's Ban on Anthropic's AI Models; Anthropic CEO Amodei on Compute Spend, Surpassing OpenAI; Google's Gemini-Powered AI Home Speaker Goes on Sale June 25.

item.title: June 16, 2026 — French Security Service to Replace Palantir With Local Firm
item.description: Today's brief leads with French Security Service to Replace Palantir With Local Firm. Also covered: Chipmaker Nvidia Seeks to Raise Over $25bn in First Bond Deal Since 2021; Europe's AI Champion Mistral Vulnerable to Russian Disinformation, Study Finds; How the AI Boom Turned a Buyout Deal Into One of History's Most Lucrative; Microsoft's Latest Surface PCs Offer Better Graphics, Faster AI; IBM's $17 Million DOJ Settlement Makes the Case for Civility.

item.title: June 16, 2026 — French Security Service to Replace Palantir With Local Firm
item.description: Today's brief leads with French Security Service to Replace Palantir With Local Firm. Also covered: Chipmaker Nvidia Seeks to Raise Over $25bn in First Bond Deal Since 2021; Europe's AI Champion Mistral Vulnerable to Russian Disinformation, Study Finds; How the AI Boom Turned a Buyout Deal Into One of History's Most Lucrative; Microsoft's Latest Surface PCs Offer Better Graphics, Faster AI; IBM's $17 Million DOJ Settlement Makes the Case for Civility.
